title: calystegia sepium var. americana
intro: Calystegia sepium var. americana, also known as American bindweed or wild morning glory, is a perennial vine native to North America and a variant of the bindweed species found in Europe. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic properties. The plant is believed to provide various health benefits, including reducing anxiety and insomnia, relieving menstrual cramps, and soothing digestive issues. Herbal preparations of Calystegia sepium var. americana often include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be made by drying the leaves and flowers of the plant.
